principle: Useful action framework: why reader comes voluntarily → what changes in their mind → how we get there = your writing plan
explanation: X.copy|useful_action: useful_action == "why_reader_comes_on_their_own"; goal == "what_changes_in_readers_mind"; tasks == "what_we_must_do_to_achieve_goal"; well-formulated_tasks == writing_plan; NO:"what_I_want_to_say" eg: useful_action: "less_micromanagement" -> goal: "reader_sees_how_motivation_system_helps" -> tasks: "examples_from_similar_businesses + theory"

principle: Two types of useful action: solve a problem or provoke an emotion — informing is NOT a useful action
explanation: X.copy|action_types: pragmatic: reader_has_problem -> searches_solution -> already_motivated; emotional: fear/outrage/curiosity/pride/nostalgia/joy; if_no_problem_AND_no_emotion -> why_read?; NO:"inform" == not_useful_action; "50_new_features" -> nobody_opens eg: "program_update_announcement" >> "5_ways_to_simplify_salon_admin_work"

principle: You can't write for everyone — segment by audience and rephrase as their specific benefit
explanation: X.copy|segment_audience: universal_needs (health/wealth/love) -> too_much_competition -> invisible_in_noise; specialization_creates_attention; examples_must_be_about_someone_specific -> reader_must_recognize_themselves; NO:one_text_for_all == text_for_none eg: "how_to_fight_procrastination" >> three_articles: "procrastination_for_students" + "for_creatives" + "in_office"

principle: Discover reader needs through observation, research, testing and intuition — then split big texts into focused ones per segment
explanation: X.copy|discover_needs: observe: support_calls + forum_questions + course_Q&A; research: interviews + surveys; test: publish_hypothesis + measure_reaction; intuition: breathe_same_air_as_audience; big_unfocused_text -> split_by_audience_into_focused_articles; NO:guessing_from_inside_the_company eg: "quarterly_blog_digest" >> "sellers_on_marketplaces:_new_laws_from_September"

principle: Pragmatic headlines must name the specific situation — no wordplay, the more concrete the more the reader feels it's about THEIR problem
explanation: X.copy|pragmatic_headline: direct + specific_situation > clever_wordplay; reader_came_to_solve_problem_fast; add: audience + time + place + method + budget; long_headline_OK_if_every_part_is_useful; NO:playful_headlines_for_problem_solving eg: "dry_and_doesnt_smell" (plumber_article) >> "how_to_find_plumber_on_Avito_if_no_time_for_search"

principle: Pragmatic text solves the problem and loses the reader — they won't remember your brand; pull questions before pushing answers
explanation: X.copy|pragmatic_limits: problem_solved -> reader_forgets_you; no_brand_loyalty_from_utility_texts; plan_accordingly; for_internal_comms: survey_first ("ask_the_CEO") -> then_answer_collected_questions; pull > push; NO:expecting_loyalty_from_one-time_problem_solving eg: "quarterly_digest" >> "ask_your_question_to_CEO" -> then_CEO_answers_in_newsletter
